source_jobs.title: Head of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
Augmodo uses Spatial AI to bridge the gap between spatial computing and retail to redefine physical commerce for the AI era. As Head of Engineering, you will be responsible for building and scaling the technical foundation of Augmodo. You will lead a team of world-class engineers in developing real-time AI-driven spatial computing applications that seamlessly integrate with the physical world.




ROLE OVERVIEW

As the Head of Engineering at Augmodo, you will serve as a cornerstone of our leadership team, responsible for nurturing the talent and technical excellence that drive our mission forward. We are looking for a seasoned people leader who understands that world-class spatial computing is built by world-class teams. Your primary focus will be to champion our engineering culture—mentoring a multi-disciplinary group of experts across CV, hardware, and cloud, and providing them with the clarity and support needed to solve the challenges of physical commerce.

In this role, you will balance high-level technical strategy with a deep commitment to organizational health. You will act as a bridge between our ambitious product vision and the day-to-day execution of your team, ensuring that as we scale our technology, we are also scaling our people, processes, and collaborative spirit. We aren't just looking for a technical expert; we are looking for a dedicated leader who takes pride in building a resilient, high-performing engineering organization where innovation and people thrive in equal measure.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead and manage a multi-disciplinary engineering team spanning computer vision, backend, frontend, hardware, and firmware.

 - Define and execute the engineering roadmap, balancing innovation with scalability.

 - Architect and build real-time, AI-driven spatial computing applications.

 - Collaborate closely with product, business, and operation teams to translate business requirements into technical solutions.

 - Oversee the development and delivery of new features and products, ensuring they meet quality standards and customer expectations.

 - Ensure the scalability, security, and reliability of Augmodo’s platform.

 - Stay at the forefront of spatial AI, computer vision, and real-time data processing.

 - Own technical decision-making, from infrastructure to ML model deployment.

 - Cultivate a culture of innovation, rapid iteration, and continuous improvement.


EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS

 - Master's in Computer Science, Robotics, or a related field.

 - 15+ years of engineering experience, with 5+ years of senior management experience, overseeing multiple engineering functions.

 - Expertise in computer vision, AI/ML, sensor fusion, or robotics.

 - Experience in full-stack development and scalable cloud architectures.

 - Passion for retail tech, AI, and transforming physical work.

 - Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to thrive in ambiguity.

 - A builder mentality—hands-on when needed, but able to scale teams and delegate effectively.

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams.


PREFERRED SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

 - Ph.D. in Computer Science, Robotics, Computer Vision, or a related field.

 - Experience with AR/MR technologies.

 - Experience in retail tech.

Augmodo offers benefits inclusive of medical, dental, vision and 401k

The salary range for this role is $225,000 USD - $235,000 USD +equity

Augmodo is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran, or disability status.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AUGMODO

Augmodo is building the "operating system for the physical shelf." We use spatial computing and wearable AI to provide real-time, store-level insights that were previously impossible to capture. We are moving fast, and our data needs to move even faster—while remaining hyper-accurate.




THE CHALLENGE

We are looking for a Software Engineer to own the logic behind our Spatial Data Ingestion & Conflation Engine. You will build the pipelines that take raw, complex data from the edge and transform it into high-fidelity insights for our customers. This role is about precision at scale: ensuring that when a brand or retailer looks at our data, it is versioned, reliable, and "production-ready."




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build Staged Pipelines: Design and maintain versioned data pipelines that handle the ingestion and conflation of complex retail data (e.g., matching computer vision detections to master product catalogs).

 - High-Fidelity Output: Ensure that data outputs surfaced to consumer-facing dashboards meet a high quality bar for accuracy and reliability.

 - Hybrid AI Strategy: Implement a "right tool for the job" approach—building robust rules-based normalization and RegEx logic for bounded tasks, while strategically integrating LLMs to solve high-complexity data matching and entity resolution.

 - Scale & Cost Management: Architect solutions that are mindful of the massive scale of retail environments, ensuring AI integrations are cost-effective and performant.

 - Versioned Data Evolution: Manage the lifecycle of data schema and pipeline logic to allow for rapid iteration without breaking downstream consumer insights.


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - The "Data Plumber" Mindset: Extensive experience building reliable, staged pipelines for complex ingestion tasks.

 - Entity Resolution & Conflation: You have experience merging disparate, messy data sources into a unified "Golden Record."

 - Pragmatic AI Experience: You are comfortable with LLMs and prompt engineering but remain skeptical enough to know when a simple regex or bounded heuristic is the better engineering choice.

 - Engineering Rigor: You believe in versioning everything—from your code to your data schemas.


BONUS POINTS

 - Physical Product Domain: Experience in retail, logistics, or supply chain (handling SKUs, UPCs, and physical inventory data).

 - Spatial Data: Experience working with data that has a physical or geographic component (GIS, LIDAR, or Computer Vision metadata).


Augmodo offers benefits inclusive of medical, dental, vision and 401k

The salary range for this role is $150,000 - 180,000 USD +equity

Augmodo is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran, or disability status.

source_jobs.title: Quality Specialist - Data Annotation
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AUGMODO

Augmodo is at the forefront of spatial computing, using AI and wearable technology to transform physical retail spaces into dynamic digital environments. Our wearable devices capture real-time 3D mapping and inventory data, empowering retail teams to work smarter while giving businesses unprecedented visibility into their physical operations.




ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Quality Specialist with Augmodo, you will be a part of a small group of individuals who will collaborate across multiple teams to define our Quality goals.

This role is about setting the quality standard, not just doing production work. You’ll be a trusted expert—the person we turn to for tough edge cases and the one who creates the benchmark data others are measured against. You'll work closely with our Computer Vision and Data Operations teams to help improve and and define our quality pipeline




AREAS OF FOCUS

 - Golden datasets & ground truth: Curate and maintain the critical benchmark datasets with our team, used to certify annotators, score vendors, and measure pipeline drift.

 - Certification & training: Create and update training materials and certification tests (measuring precision and speed) required for vendor and annotator onboarding.

 - Drift monitoring & scoring: Periodically evaluate annotators against benchmark data to identify drift and help manage QA metrics and scoring models for the Data Ops Hub.

 - Quality audits: Conduct rigorous, double-blind quality audits to ensure a gold standard across computer-vision datasets.

 - Production labeling: Serve as a high-trust annotator to directly handle production labeling when critical (P0) needs arise.


SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience: 2+ years in data annotation and quality assurance, including reviewing and calibrating other annotators' work.

 - Technical Tooling: Experience with Encord or equivalent enterprise annotation platforms (e.g., CVAT, Labelbox, SuperAnnotate); comfort with complex 2D/3D annotation (bounding boxes, polygons, masks).

 - Judgment & Attention to Detail: Exceptional ability to spot, track, and adjudicate out-of-stock products and edge cases across large video datasets — and to define a defensible standard for them.

 - Communication: Strong verbal and written skills to document standards clearly, justify quality decisions, and collaborate with a global, cross-functional team.


WHY JOIN AUGMODO?

 - Ownership: You set the standard the entire labeling operation is measured against — not a cog in the pipeline, but the team that defines it.

 - Impact: Help build the world's first passive inventory tracking system for the "Augmented Workforce," and the quality system that lets it scale.

 - Innovation: Work at the cutting edge of Spatial AI with a venture-backed startup scaling globally.

 - Flexibility: Join a remote-first culture that values transparency, speed, and precision.

Augmodo offers benefits inclusive of medical, dental, vision and 401k

The salary range for this role is $45,000 USD - $65,000 USD +equity

Augmodo is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran, or disability status.

notes: Small team with strong focus on engineering and AI-driven spatial computing applications.
